title: The DAO — reentrancy drains $60M ETH; led to the ETH/ETC fork
note: Recursive call to splitDAO before balance update. Fix by hard-fork; chain split into ETH (rollback) and ETC (immutable). Attacker address never publicly attributed.

title: Parity Multisig — initWallet exploit drains 153k ETH (~$30M)
note: Vulnerable initWallet function let any address claim ownership of a deployed multisig. Three high-profile multisig wallets were drained for 153,037 ETH total: aeternity (82,189 ETH), Edgeless (26,793 ETH), Swarm City (44,055 ETH). Attacker EOA labeled 'Multisig Exploit Hacker' on Etherscan.

title: Parity library suicide bricks ~$300M of ETH permanently
note: User devops199 first called initWallet on the uninitialized WalletLibrary (0x863df6bf...), making 0xae7168... the sole owner, then called kill, self-destructing the shared library that 587 multisig wallets delegated to. ~513,774 ETH frozen permanently. EIP-999 to restore the contract was rejected by community vote.

title: bZx — first major flashloan attack on DeFi (~$355k)
note: First widely covered flashloan exploit. Attacker borrowed 10,000 ETH on dYdX, manipulated WBTC/ETH price on Uniswap V1 via a leveraged short on bZx Fulcrum, and arbitraged WBTC borrowed from Compound for 1,193 ETH (~$298k) profit. A second similar attack on 2020-02-18 netted another ~$633k. Attacker funded via Tornado Cash and never publicly attributed.

title: dForce / Lendf.Me — imBTC reentrancy drains $25M (later returned)
note: imBTC's ERC-777 tokensToSend callback let the attacker reenter Lendf.Me's supply() after withdraw(), restoring their pre-withdraw balance and enabling exponential drains. ~$25M lost across WETH, WBTC, USDx, USDT, USDC, etc. Attacker doxxed and returned all funds within ~4 days.

title: Harvest Finance — Curve oracle manipulation drains $24M
note: Manipulated USDC and USDT prices in the Curve y pool via flashloan to inflate fUSDC/fUSDT mint rates, then redeemed for arbitrage profit across many iterations. Attacker EOA 0xf224ab... is labeled 'Harvest.Finance: Hacker 1' on Etherscan; was funded via Tornado Cash.

title: Pickle Finance — evil jar swap drains $19.7M from cDAI strategy
note: Pickle ControllerV4.swapExactJarForJar() lacked authentication and Jar-whitelist validation, letting the attacker swap real cDAI (held by the legitimate pDAI Jar) into a malicious lookalike Jar they deployed, then redeem for 19.76M DAI. Attacker funded via Tornado Cash.

title: Yearn v1 yDAI — $11M lost via Curve 3pool imbalance flashloan
note: Attacker used an Aave flashloan to repeatedly imbalance and rebalance Curve's 3pool while forcing the v1 yDAI vault to deposit/withdraw at unfavorable rates, skimming ~$2.8M profit from $11M of vault losses. Attacker EOA labeled 'Yearn (yDai) Exploiter' on Etherscan.

title: Poly Network — cross-chain bridge drained for $611M; attacker returned all funds
note: Manipulated EthCrossChainManager (0x838bf9e9...) to override the keeper public key stored in EthCrossChainData, then minted unauthorized withdrawals from the LockProxy/Asset Proxy (0x250e7698...). Attacker EOA 0xc8a65fad... per rekt.news. Attacker eventually returned the entire amount. Hit Ethereum, BSC, and Polygon.

title: Compound — proposal 062 misallocates $90M+ COMP via comptroller bug
note: Not an attack — a governance upgrade introduced a logic bug in the Comptroller that paid out far more COMP than intended. Some recipients refused to return funds. No single attacker — many opportunistic recipients.

title: Cream Finance — yUSDVault price manipulation drains $130M
note: Third major Cream exploit in 2021. Manipulated yUSDVault.pricePerShare via direct token transfers to bloat collateral value, then borrowed against fake collateral. Two coordinated attacker EOAs (Address A 0x961d... + Address B 0x2435...) per rekt.news + ImmuneBytes.

title: BadgerDAO — front-end exploit drains $120M via injected approve tx
note: Attacker injected malicious script into Badger's Cloudflare-served front-end, prompting users to sign unlimited token approvals on the attacker's address. Attacker EOA 0x1fcdb04d... per rekt.news. Attack started 00:00:23 UTC 2021-12-02.

title: Wormhole — signature verification bypass mints 120k wETH on Solana
note: Attacker spoofed a guardian signature set on the Solana side, minted 120k wETH unbacked, then bridged to Ethereum. Jump Crypto refilled the bridge. Attacker EOA confirmed by rekt.news.

title: Ronin Bridge — $625M Lazarus Group validator-key compromise
note: 5 of 9 Ronin validators compromised via spear-phishing. Attacker signed off on fraudulent withdrawals from the Ethereum-side bridge contract 0x1a2a1c93... per rekt.news. Attributed to DPRK Lazarus Group.

title: Inverse Finance — Keep3r oracle manipulation drains $15.6M
note: Manipulated INV/ETH price on SushiSwap (Keep3r oracle) via a large swap using the attacker's own 500 ETH (not a flashloan, bypassing the TWAP flash-loan guard), then borrowed ETH/WBTC/DOLA/YFI on Anchor against inflated INV collateral. Attacker EOA 0x8b4c1083... labeled 'Inverse Finance Exploiter 2' on Etherscan; funded from Tornado.Cash 2022-04-02 07:22 UTC.

title: Beanstalk — governance flashloan drains $182M via emergencyCommit
note: Attacker took a flashloan to acquire majority governance, then passed and instantly executed a malicious proposal that drained the protocol's reserves. Beanstalk farms diamond at 0xc1e088fc... is the victim contract.

title: Harmony Horizon Bridge — $100M drained via 2-of-5 multisig key compromise
note: Attacker compromised 2 of 5 multisig signers controlling the Ethereum-side bridge 0xf9fb1c50... Lazarus Group attribution by US Treasury (Jan 2023). Attacker EOA 0x0d043128... per rekt.news.

title: Nomad Bridge — $190M drained in copy-paste free-for-all
note: Init bug treated unsigned messages as valid. Once the first attacker (0x56d8b635... — Nomad Bridge Exploiter 1 per Etherscan label) showed the way, hundreds of opportunists ran the same tx with their own address — the attack became a public free-for-all. Victim is the Nomad ERC20 Bridge contract.

title: Wintermute — $160M drained from market-maker hot wallet via Profanity vanity-address grind
note: Wintermute used a vanity address generated by the Profanity tool, which had been disclosed as having a guessable seed. Attacker brute-forced the private key. Attacker EOA 0xe74b28c2... (Wintermute Exploiter, Etherscan); compromised Wintermute admin/hot wallet 0x0000000fe6a5... per multiple post-mortems (Halborn, CoinDesk).

title: BNB Chain — Token Hub IAVL proof forgery mints $586M of BNB
note: Forged a Merkle proof against a vulnerable IAVL implementation, minting 2M BNB cross-chain. Validators paused the chain mid-attack to limit damage. Only ~$137M moved off-chain before the halt. Attacker EOA 0x489a8756... per rekt.news + Nansen + Halborn.

title: Mango Markets — Avraham Eisenberg oracle manipulation, $114M (Solana, NOT EVM — informational)
note: INFORMATIONAL ONLY — Solana, will be filtered out at seed time. Listed for completeness because it's referenced widely.

title: Euler — donateToReserves donation accounting bug drains $197M
note: donateToReserves let the attacker forfeit collateral while keeping liability, triggering an under-collateralized liquidation against themselves at a profit. Attacker returned 100% of funds after months of negotiation.

title: Multichain — MPC keys compromised, $231M drained from bridges
note: MPC node keys controlled by Multichain's CEO were compromised after his arrest in China. Funds drained from multiple bridge contracts across chains (rekt.news names ~6 attacker EOAs holding the loot, but they're per-chain dust addresses rather than a single canonical attacker).

title: Curve — Vyper compiler reentrancy bug drains $73M from stable pools
note: Vyper versions 0.2.15-0.3.0 had a faulty reentrancy lock. Multiple Curve pools (alETH, msETH, pETH, CRV/ETH) drained on July 30, 2023. White-hat MEV bots front-ran several attacks; some funds returned. 0x6ec21d18... is labeled 'Frontrunner (JPEGd_69 Exploit)' on Etherscan — an MEV bot that beat the original attacker but kept the ~$11M proceeds; 0xdce5d6b4... is the alETH/ETH pool drainer.

title: Mixin — $200M drained via cloud database breach exposing hot wallet keys
note: Cloud-service-provider DB breach exposed Mixin's deposit hot-wallet keys; only ~50% of user assets were guaranteed by the founder post-incident. Funds drained on Ethereum and Bitcoin. Three attacker EOAs labeled 'Mixin Network Exploiter 1/2/3' on Etherscan, per rekt.news.

title: KyberSwap Elastic — concentrated-liquidity tick exploit drains $48M
note: Attacker exploited an off-by-one in tick boundary handling to repeatedly cross the same liquidity range, double-counting liquidity. Largest hit was Arbitrum (>$20M of $48M total) — primary chain reclassified accordingly. Attacker EOA 0x50275e0b... per rekt.news.

title: PlayDapp — minter role compromise mints 1.79B PLA tokens (~$290M)
note: Attacker added themselves as a minter on the PLA token contract (0x3a4f40...), then minted ~1.79B PLA across two waves (200M on Feb 9, 1.59B on Feb 12) and dumped on exchanges. Exchanges froze most proceeds; only ~$32M of the $290M was successfully laundered. Two attacker EOAs documented in Nefture / Halborn post-mortems.

title: Munchables — rogue dev drains $62M; funds returned 24h later
note: An insider on the Munchables team (suspected DPRK affiliation) inserted a backdoor into the contract during development. After negotiation, funds were returned and no users lost money. Rogue dev EOA 0x6e8836f0... per rekt.news.

title: Gala Games — admin key compromise mints 5B GALA tokens (~$200M)
note: Compromised dormant minter account (idle ~180 days) on the GALA ERC-20 contract (0xd1d2eb1b...) was used to mint 5B GALA. Gala blocklisted the attacker mid-attack; ~592M GALA had been swapped for ~5,912 ETH (~$22M), which was later returned to Gala's cold wallet. Attacker EOA labeled 'Gala Games Exploiter' on Etherscan.

title: LI.FI — facet upgrade exploit drains $11M from approval users
note: Attacker exploited an unverified GasZipFacet in the LiFi diamond contract (0x1231deb6...) to call arbitrary functions on users who'd granted unlimited approvals. Attacker EOA per LI.FI security incident report + QuillAudits + BlockSec.

title: Penpie — PendleStaking reentrancy via fake market drains $27M
note: batchHarvestMarketRewards on the PendleStaking implementation (0xFF51c6b4...) lacked a reentrancy guard. Attacker registered a fake Pendle market with a malicious SY token, then reentered depositMarket during the redeemRewards callback to repeatedly inflate their reward balance. Three malicious txs between 18:35 and 18:42 UTC drained ~$27M; Pendle paused at 18:45 UTC. Attacker EOA labeled 'Penpiexyz Exploiter' on Etherscan; funded ~10 ETH from Tornado.Cash hours earlier and ultimately laundered proceeds back through Tornado.

title: DeltaPrime — admin key compromise mints 10^69 dpTokens for ~$6M
note: Compromised admin key (0x40e4ff9e...) upgraded DeltaPrime Blue's proxy contracts on Arbitrum to a malicious implementation (0xD4CA224a...), then minted >1.1e69 of DPUSDC/DPARB/DPBTCb/DPWETH and redeemed them for the underlying. ~$5.98M drained, swapped to ETH, bridged to mainnet, and routed through Tornado.Cash. DPRK affiliation suspected but not confirmed at the time of disclosure.

title: Onyx Protocol — Compound v2 fork empty-market rounding bug, $3.8M
note: Same empty-market exchange-rate bug that bit Onyx for $2.1M in November 2023 — a known Compound v2 fork issue with low-decimal cTokens — exploited again on a freshly listed oVUSD market via a Balancer flashloan. Attacker EOA 0x680910cf... labeled 'OnyxDAO Exploiter 1' on Etherscan; attack contract 0xAE7d68b1... Loss: 4.1M VUSD, 7.35M XCN, 5K DAI, 0.23 WBTC, 50K USDT (~$3.8M total).

title: Radiant Capital — multisig compromise drains $50M cross-chain
note: DPRK-attributed (UNC4736 per Mandiant): 3 of 11 multisig signers were tricked by malware that displayed legitimate Gnosis Safe transaction data while actually routing a malicious LendingPoolAddressesProvider upgrade to their hardware wallets. The compromised Arbitrum admin multisig (0x111CEEee...) executed the upgrade at 17:09:18 UTC; the BSC admin multisig (0xE4714D6B...) followed at 17:11 UTC. Malicious contracts were also deployed on ETH and Base but never executed.

title: Bybit — Safe{Wallet} signing-UI compromise drains $1.43B in ETH/stETH/cmETH/mETH
note: Largest crypto theft on record. DPRK / TraderTraitor (Lazarus Group) attribution per FBI IC3 PSA 250226 (Feb 26 2025) + Chainalysis + the Safe + Mandiant joint forensic. Not a smart contract bug — attackers compromised a Safe{Wallet} developer's macOS machine, hijacked active AWS session tokens to bypass MFA, and modified the malicious JavaScript bundle in Safe{Wallet}'s S3-hosted frontend two days before the attack. The payload was scoped to fire only when Bybit's ETH cold wallet (0x1Db92e2E...) was the signing context — every other Safe user saw a clean UI. Signers reviewed what looked like a routine transfer in their wallet UIs; the actual signed call was a delegateCall to an attacker-controlled contract (0x96221423...) that overwrote storage slot 0 of the Safe proxy, swapping the legitimate Safe singleton (0x34CfAC64...) for an attacker-deployed singleton (0xbDd077f6...) with a hidden sweepERC20() function. Attacker then drained 401,346 ETH + 90,375 stETH + 15,000 cmETH + 8,000 mETH (~$1.43B at the time) and laundered through THORChain → BTC. Malicious approval tx 0xb61413c4... (block timestamp 2025-02-21 14:16:11 UTC). Attacker EOA 0x47666Fab... labeled 'Bybit Exploiter' on Etherscan. Bybit absorbed the loss without halting customer withdrawals, closing the balance-sheet gap via bridge financing within ~72h. Safe{Wallet} fully rebuilt infrastructure and rotated all credentials per the Mandiant-co-authored forensic. Triggered industry-wide review of multisig signing-UI assumptions.

title: Abracadabra — GM Cauldron solvency-check bypass via failed GMX orders, $13M
note: Five GM (GMX V2) Cauldrons on Arbitrum drained over 56 txs spanning 07:57:52–09:37:36 UTC. Failed GMX deposits left collateral parked in the OrderAgent while the cauldron's bookkeeping treated the position as solvent, letting the attacker self-liquidate against phantom collateral and walk with a bad loan. Borrowing on all gmCauldrons paused 09:46:22 UTC. Net loss ~6,262 ETH (~$13.4M); proceeds bridged Arbitrum → Ethereum. Funding/laundering EOA 0xAF9e33Aa... per rekt.news.

title: Cork Protocol — CorkHook missing access control mints fake DS/CT, drains 3,761 wstETH
note: beforeSwap on CorkHook lacked access control, letting a malicious caller pass crafted hookData into CorkCall to split the protocol's own weETH8DS-2 tokens into fake_DS / fake_CT against the wstETH:weETH market. Redeeming the fake tokens drained 3,761.878 wstETH, swapped to 4,530 ETH (~$12M) within 16 minutes. Attacker EOA 0xEA6f30e3... labeled 'Cork Protocol Exploiter 1' on Etherscan. Protocol had passed four audits, including two contests.

title: Resupply — ERC-4626 first-donation attack on freshly deployed cvcrvUSD vault, $9.6M
note: Classic ERC-4626 first-depositor attack on a cvcrvUSD ResupplyPair (0x6e90c85a...) deployed only ~2 hours earlier. Attacker took a $4K USDC flashloan from Morpho, donated 2,000 crvUSD into the empty vault, minted 1 wei of shares, and let integer division round the borrow exchange rate to zero — letting them deposit 1 wei of crcrvUSD as collateral and borrow ~$10M of reUSD. Net ~$9.56M, swapped to ETH, split across two addresses, washed through Tornado.Cash. Attacker EOA labeled 'Resupply Exploiter 1' on Etherscan.

title: GMX V1 — executeDecreaseOrder reentrancy desyncs GLP pricing, $42M
note: Reentrancy-like flaw in PositionManager.executeDecreaseOrder() on GMX V1 (Arbitrum) let the attacker desync globalShortAveragePrices vs. globalShortSizes mid-call, inflating GLP price within a single tx. The fix that introduced this bug was itself shipped to patch a 2022 finding — and went unaudited. Attacker EOA labeled 'GMX Exploiter 1' on Arbiscan; settled the white-hat path: returned ~$37M to the GMX multisig (0x8D1d2e24...) and kept a $5M bounty.

title: Balancer V2 — _upscaleArray rounding error compounds across batchSwap, $128M
note: Rounding bug in _upscaleArray combined with crafted batchSwap calls let the attacker drive pool balances into the 8–9 wei range, where Solidity integer division rounded ~10% per swap. The exploit ran 65+ micro-swaps inside the attack contract's constructor — drained $128.64M of WETH/osETH/wstETH from Balancer V2 boosted pools across six chains in under 30 minutes. Balancer V3 unaffected. Attacker EOA 0x506D1f9E... labeled 'Balancer Exploiter 1' on Etherscan; deployed attack contract 0x54B53503... Berachain halted its network and emergency hard-forked to contain BEX (Balancer V2 fork) damage.

title: Resolv — compromised SERVICE_ROLE key mints 80M unbacked USR for ~$300K USDC, $25M extracted
note: Off-chain infrastructure compromise, not a smart contract bug. Attackers pivoted from a contractor's GitHub credentials into Resolv's AWS KMS environment and altered access policies to gain control of the SERVICE_ROLE signing EOA (0x15CAd41e6BdCaDc7121ce65080489C92CF6de398). The two-step USR mint flow trusted that key to set the minted amount as an unchecked parameter via completeSwap on the USR Counter (0xa27a69Ae...). With ~$100K-$300K USDC deposited across three transactions, the attacker minted ~80M USR, swapped to ~11,400 ETH (~$25M) on Curve before USR's peg collapsed (low ~$0.025 on USR/USDC, 17 minutes after the first 50M mint). First malicious completeSwap tx 0xfe37f25e... at 02:21:35 UTC. Attacker EOAs labeled 'Resolv Exploiter' (0x04A288a7...) and 'Resolv Exploiter 2' (0x8ED8cF0C...) on Etherscan; 0xb945eC1b... funded Exploiter 2 with 524 ETH. Resolv paused the protocol, burned hacker-held wstUSR/stUSR balances via contract upgrade, and published the post-mortem on April 4, 2026. Knock-on impact: Euler disabled USR/RLP collateral; Gauntlet USDC Core had ~$5M wstUSR/USDC exposure.

title: Kelp DAO — LayerZero 1-of-1 DVN forged message mints 116,500 unbacked rsETH
note: DPRK-attributed (TraderTraitor / Lazarus per LayerZero + Chainalysis). Not a smart contract bug — attackers compromised internal RPC nodes feeding LayerZero's DVN and DDoS'd the public ones, forcing the verifier to trust a forged cross-chain message under a 1-of-1 DVN configuration. The forged message released 116,500 rsETH (~$292M, ~18% of supply) from Kelp's LayerZero bridge into attacker control. Kelp paused contracts to block a follow-up ~$95M theft; Arbitrum Security Council and law enforcement froze ~30K ETH downstream. Attacker laundered via THORChain → BTC. Attack tx 0x1ae232da... at 17:35 UTC against LayerZero EndpointV2 (0x1a440760...). Triggered emergency rsETH freezes across Aave, SparkLend, Fluid, and Upshift.
